note: seq=28: v4 PULLER SELF-UPGRADE jumped to FRONT of queue (was seq-32) -- v4 is the fix for stuck queues, so it must not sit behind one. fetch->sha-pin->--selftest gate->atomic install->restart timer; lanes ship OFF (ATLAS_LANES opt-in); v3 restore-point/health-gate auto-rolls-back a bad v4. After this, lifelines/metrics get their own lane and can never be blocked by a data seq again.

steps.note: seq=32: v3->v4 PULLER SELF-UPGRADE (lane decoupling). v4 is backward-compatible: drains the flat seq-N queue byte-for-byte like v3 (this manifest lands via that path); reads per-lane queues only when ATLAS_LANES is set (default OFF -> v4==v3). Guardrails+rollback identical to v3. Wrapped by v3 restore-point->health-check->AUTO-ROLLBACK, so a bad v4 reverts to v3 automatically.

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import hashlib,sys; p='/opt/atlas/staging/atlas-autopull.v4.sh'; want='5d5a5ae8bb45ecf96429632aba7a86e7bcb6bd0478d783fb82f9df742b9df1eb'; got=hashlib.sha256(open(p,'rb').read()).hexdigest(); sys.exit(0 if got==want else 'SHA MISMATCH got='+got)"
